title: oxalis furcata
intro: Oxalis furcata, also known as the Wood Sorrel or Sourgrass, is a perennial plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and leaves that have a sour taste due to their high oxalic acid content. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and rheumatism. Oxalis furcata is also rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting overall health. In herbal preparations, Oxalis furcata is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be used as a natural remedy for sore throats and mouth sores.
